title: Unit 1 Test: Addition and Subtraction within 1,000

problems.question: What is the next number? 5, 10, 15, 20, ___

feedback.correct: Great job! The pattern adds 5 each time.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is C (25). The rule is add 5.

hints: Find the difference between two next numbers — what's the rule?
problems.feedback_correct: Great job! The pattern adds 5 each time.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: What is the missing number? 14, 21, 28, ___, 42

feedback.incorrect: The answer is 35. The rule is add 7.

hints: Find the difference: 21-14=7.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: What is 48 rounded to the nearest ten?

feedback.incorrect: The answer is C (50). 48 is closer to 50 than to 40.

hints: Look at the ones digit. Is it 5 or more?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Round 362 to the nearest hundred.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is 400. Since the tens digit is 6, round up.

hints: Look at the tens place. 6 means round up.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Estimate the sum by rounding to the nearest ten: 47 + 32.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is B (80). 47 rounds to 50, 32 rounds to 30.

hints: Round each number to the nearest ten first, then add.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Rounding 196 to the nearest hundred gives 200.

feedback.correct: Correct! 196 is closer to 200 than to 100.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is True. 196 rounds up to 200.

hints: Look at the tens digit (9). 5 or more rounds up.
problems.feedback_correct: Correct! 196 is closer to 200 than to 100.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Use mental math to add: 230 + 140 = ___

feedback.incorrect: The answer is 370. Think 23 tens + 14 tens = 37 tens.

hints: Add tens: 23 + 14 = 37, then add a zero.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Which shows the Commutative Property of Addition?

feedback.correct: Right! Order doesn't change the sum.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is B. Commutative = same sum, different order.

hints: Commutative means changing the ORDER of two numbers.
problems.feedback_correct: Right! Order doesn't change the sum.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Which shows the Associative Property of Addition?

feedback.correct: Great! Grouping doesn't change the sum.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is C. Associative changes the grouping.

hints: Associative means changing the GROUPING with parentheses.
problems.feedback_correct: Great! Grouping doesn't change the sum.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Break apart to add: 248 + 136 = ___

feedback.incorrect: The answer is 384. Break each number into hundreds, tens, ones.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Add using place value: 356 + 287 = ___

feedback.incorrect: The answer is 643. Remember to regroup when a column totals 10 or more.

hints: Add ones first, regroup. Then tens, then hundreds.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: What is 425 + 199?

feedback.incorrect: The answer is B (624).

hints: 199 is close to 200. Try 425 + 200, then subtract 1.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Estimate the difference by rounding to the nearest hundred: 687 − 312.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is B (400). 687 rounds to 700, 312 rounds to 300.

hints: 687 rounds to 700, 312 rounds to 300. Subtract.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: An estimate of 512 − 248 rounded to the nearest hundred is 300.
hints: 512 rounds to 500, 248 rounds to 200. 500 − 200 = ?

problems.question: Use mental math: 460 − 130 = ___

feedback.incorrect: The answer is 330. Think 46 tens − 13 tens = 33 tens.

hints: Subtract tens: 46 − 13 = 33, then add a zero.

problems.question: Subtract using place value: 532 − 148 = ___

feedback.incorrect: The answer is 384. Regroup when the top digit is smaller.

hints: Regroup a ten into ones, then a hundred into tens.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: What is 700 − 256?

feedback.incorrect: The answer is B (444). Regroup across the zeros.

hints: Subtract by counting up from 256 to 700, or use place values.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Combine place values to subtract: 603 − 247 = ___

feedback.incorrect: The answer is 356. Combine hundreds and tens to regroup across zeros.

hints: Think of 603 as 59 tens + 13 ones.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test

problems.question: A library has 345 fiction books and 218 non-fiction books. How many books in total?

feedback.incorrect: The answer is C (563). Add the two amounts.

hints: Total means add. Line up the place values.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Mia had 420 stickers. She gave 175 to friends. How many does she have left?

feedback.incorrect: The answer is 245. Subtract: 420 − 175.

hints: Subtract what she gave away from what she started with.

problems.question: A store had 500 apples. They sold 180 in the morning and 145 in the afternoon. How many apples are left?
hints: First add what was sold, then subtract from 500.

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: The equation 9 + 0 = 9 shows the Identity Property of Addition.

feedback.correct: Correct! Adding 0 doesn't change the number.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is True. Identity: any number + 0 = itself.

hints: Identity Property: adding 0 keeps the number the same.
problems.feedback_correct: Correct! Adding 0 doesn't change the number.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Sarah says 500−263=247. Tim says 500−263=237. Who is correct?
hints: Calculate it yourself.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is B (Tim). Read the problem twice. Identify what's given and what's asked, then choose an operation.

pretest.question: Which strategy is best for 601 − 598?
choices: A) Take away tens and ones
solution_steps: The numbers are very close (only 3 apart)
hints: When numbers are very close, counting up is quickest.

feedback.correct: Correct! The answer is C (Count up).
feedback.incorrect: The answer is C (Count up). Read the problem twice. Identify what's given and what's asked, then choose an operation.

pretest.feedback_correct: Correct! The answer is C (Count up).

hints: Subtract first, then add.

pretest.question: Estimate 145 + 358. Which method gives an estimate closest to the exact answer (503)?

A.note: Rounded 145 down to 100 — gets 500 by luck

D.note: 100 is not a good compatible number for 145

hints: Which replacement numbers are closest to the originals?

feedback.incorrect: The answer is C (Compatible: 150 + 350 = 500). Round each number first, then do the operation.

pretest.question: Jake's class collected 412 cans. Emma's class collected 389 cans. Together they gave 215 cans to charity. How many are left for recycling?
hints: Combine both classes, then subtract charity donation.

feedback.correct: Nice work! The answer is B (586).
feedback.incorrect: The answer is B (586). Read the problem twice. Identify what's given and what's asked, then choose an operation.

pretest.question: Five friends collected stickers: Ana 45, Ben 78, Cal 55, Dee 22, Eve 100. They want to count the total quickly. What is the best way to group them?
choices: D) 100+78+55+45+22 = just start with the biggest

B.note: Works but not the 'best' mental math strategy

D.note: Starting with the biggest doesn't make friendly pairs

solution_steps: This is the easiest mental math approach
hints: Which pairs of numbers add up to exactly 100?

feedback.incorrect: The answer is A ((45+55) + (78+22) + 100 = 100+100+100 = 300). Read the problem twice. Identify what's given and what's asked, then choose an operation.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/L5_use_properties_to_add
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: What is the GREATEST whole number that rounds to 800 when rounded to the nearest hundred?
solution_steps: Step 1: Numbers that round to 800: from 750 to 849.
Step 2: 850 has tens=5 and rounds UP to 900.
Step 3: So the greatest number is 849.

feedback.correct: 849 has tens=4 and rounds DOWN to 800. 850 would round UP to 900. So 849 is the greatest!
feedback.incorrect: 850 has tens=5 and rounds UP to 900. The biggest number rounding to 800 is 849 (tens=4).

pretest.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What does greatest mean? Read 2: What hundred does it round to? Read 3: What digit makes it switch?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/L2_round_nearest_ten_hundred
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: What is the biggest number that still rounds DOWN to 800? What happens at 850?
pretest.feedback_correct: 849 has tens=4 and rounds DOWN to 800. 850 would round UP to 900. So 849 is the greatest!

pretest.question: Estimate 622 − 307 by rounding to the nearest hundred.
hints: Round to hundreds, then subtract.

feedback.correct: Good job! The answer is B (300).
feedback.incorrect: The answer is B (300). Look at the tens digit. If it's 5 or more, round up; otherwise round down.

pretest.feedback_correct: Good job! The answer is B (300).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/L8_estimate_differences
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Show two different strategies to solve 199 + 364. Which gives the same answer?
choices: B) Friendly: 200+364=564. Break apart: 100+300=400, 99+64=163, 400+163=563. Different answers.
D) Both strategies always give different answers.

C.note: Added 1 instead of subtracting — wrong adjustment

D.note: All strategies should give the same correct answer

hints: Try both strategies carefully. Do you get the same number?

feedback.incorrect: The answer is A (Friendly: 200+364=564, 564-1=563. Break apart: 100+300=400, 99+64=163, 400+163=563. Both = 563.). Read the problem twice. Identify what's given and what's asked, then choose an operation.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.1 Lesson — U1_add_sub_1000/L4_mental_math_addition
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
